name: ExtensionNutritionIntake_IngredientLabel
title: R5: Total nutrient for the whole meal, product, serving (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `NutritionIntake.ingredientLabel` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`NutritionIntake.ingredientLabel` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `NutritionIntake.ingredientLabel` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `NutritionIntake.ingredientLabel` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionIntake` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionIntake.ingredientLabel` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Total nutrient for the whole meal, product, serving (new)
element.comment:
Element `NutritionIntake.ingredientLabel` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionIntake` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionIntake.ingredientLabel` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Individual item nutrients are not currently included in the resource and will likely end up as a reference in nutritionProduct to represent the individual items.

Element `NutritionIntake.ingredientLabel.nutrient` is part of an existing definition because parent element `NutritionIntake.ingredientLabel` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `NutritionIntake.ingredientLabel.nutrient` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionIntake` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionIntake.ingredientLabel.nutrient` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
